one_liner: WooCommerce (self-hosted WordPress plugin) offers a full REST API for store management authenticated via Consumer Key/Secret (Basic Auth or OAuth1.0a), with self-serve key generation in the WP admin and emerging native MCP support.

auth_methods.notes: HTTPS sites use HTTP Basic Auth with Consumer Key as username and Consumer Secret as password; can also be passed as query string params.
Non-HTTPS sites use OAuth 1.0a 'one-legged' authentication with signature, timestamp, and nonce (not OAuth2, but closest category).
Consumer Key/Secret pairs generated via WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API, or auto-generated via the /wc-auth/v1/authorize browser-based flow for third-party app integration.

self_serve_notes: WooCommerce is free, open-source software with no platform-based API pricing. Any store admin can self-generate Consumer Key/Secret API keys directly from the WordPress admin dashboard (WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API) or via the /wc-auth/v1/authorize endpoint for third-party app flows. No approval process required since it's self-hosted, though a live store/server is needed.

api_surface.notes: Two REST APIs: authenticated REST API v3 for full management of store data (products, orders, coupons, customers, etc.) and an unauthenticated Store API v1 for customer-facing frontend features.

main_blocker: None significant for self-hosted stores; REST API and self-serve key generation are fully available today. Native MCP support is still gated behind a feature flag (mcp_integration) and depends on self-hosting WordPress/WooCommerce with the Abilities API/MCP adapter, so per-store setup effort varies. Headless-specific tooling may require premium add-ons in newer versions.

evidence.supports: Describes Basic Auth via Consumer Key/Secret, OAuth 1.0a for non-HTTPS, and self-serve key generation in WP admin plus auto-generation via /wc-auth/v1/authorize.
Full REST API v3 documentation covering coupons, customers, orders, products, etc., confirming broad REST API surface.
Confirms WooCommerce core software and API access is free/open-source with no platform-based pricing tiers.
Details on REST API v3 as primary authenticated API surface.

flags: MCP support is behind a feature flag (mcp_integration) and depends on WordPress Abilities API/MCP adapter setup, so maturity/stability is uncertain.
Community-maintained MCP servers exist but native support is very recent and evidence is limited to search summaries without direct doc scraping.
Headless-specific integration tools reportedly moved to premium third-party packages in recent versions, per one search source (ecommerce-times.com), which may affect cost for advanced use cases.
Self-hosted nature means 'self-serve' applies per-store; there's no centralized WooCommerce.com platform API key issuance for arbitrary third-party apps without each store owner performing setup.
